title: 7.2 Struggle for Economic Independence
description: 30 questions covering Africa's struggle for economic independence, regional economic communities, and debt crisis

questions.question: From which years did Africa experience substantial economic growth?
After the period of substantial growth, what trend did the African economy follow?
In the 1980s and 1990s, which two principal factors affected African economies?
Since the 1960s, raw material exports from Africa dropped in price compared to manufactured imports by how much?
To which financial institutions did African governments turn for emergency loans?
options: World Bank and African Development Bank

questions.question: The IMF and World Bank were financed primarily by:
What does REC stand for?
RECs were established for what purpose?
The Abuja Treaty of 1991 established which organization?
The Lagos Plan of Action for the Development of Africa was adopted in:
COMESA was formed in which month and year?
COMESA replaced which earlier organization?
How many member states did COMESA have? (From Tunisia to Swaziland)
COMESA stands for:
options: Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa
Council for Economic and Social Affairs
Committee for Eastern and Southern African States
Commission for Economic and Security Affairs
questions.correctAnswer: Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa

questions.question: COMESA's member states span from which country to which?
ECOWAS is the regional economic community for:
ECOWAS stands for:
options: Economic Community of West African States
European Council of West African States
Economic Cooperation of Western African States
Eastern Community of West African States
questions.correctAnswer: Economic Community of West African States

questions.question: IGAD includes which countries?
options: Ethiopia, Sudan, South Sudan, Eritrea, Kenya, Uganda, Djibouti
questions.correctAnswer: Ethiopia, Sudan, South Sudan, Eritrea, Kenya, Uganda, Djibouti

questions.question: IGAD stands for:
SADC is the regional economic community for:
SADC stands for:
options: Southern Alliance for Development and Cooperation

questions.question: The Abuja Treaty was signed in which year?
The Lagos Plan of Action was adopted by which organization?
Raw material export prices for Africa dropped by how much compared to manufactured imports since the 1960s?
The Preferential Trade Area (PTA) was established in:
The PTA was replaced by which organization?
What term describes the period of substantial economic growth followed by a downward trend for Africa?
options: Growth then decline (1965-1974 growth, then downward trend)
questions.correctAnswer: Growth then decline (1965-1974 growth, then downward trend)

questions.question: The Abuja Treaty established the AEC. What does AEC stand for?
In the 1980s and 1990s, which TWO factors hindered African economic development?
Why did African governments turn to IMF and World Bank for loans?
options: For emergency loans due to economic crisis
questions.correctAnswer: For emergency loans due to economic crisis
